title: Mauranthemum reboudianum
intro: Mauranthemum reboudianum, also known as Mountain Everlasting, is a flowering plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its long-lasting, pinkish-white flowers. The plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Mauranthemum reboudianum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Mauranthemum reboudianum is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to soothe digestive issues and promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Mauranthemum reboudianum is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This plant is further classified within the genus Mauranthemum, a small group of species native to the Mediterranean region. Mauranthemum reboudianum is also classified as a species within the order Asterales, which includes various flowering plants with similar characteristics. The classification of Mauranthemum reboudianum is based on its morphological and anatomical features, as well as its genetic makeup. This classification system helps to understand the evolutionary relationships among different plant species.
